methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA demonstrates institutional commitment to building distributed AI literacy through community-embedded education infrastructure, potentially creating a replicable model for peer-driven capability building beyond traditional tech hubs.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia signals grassroots capacity building in historically underserved regions, testing whether deep technical capability can take root through place-based initiatives rather than metro concentration.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA school systems wrestling with AI integration reveals the metro advantage in early adoption, but their struggle to define pedagogical strategy suggests even wealthy districts face the shallow-vs-deep capability choice that will shape generational outcomes.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying AI-powered home search tools exemplifies platform-delivered AI creating broad but shallow adoption—users gain capability without understanding, reinforcing the consumer model over builder literacy.

signals.title: New AI training program launches to develop national security leaders - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: Virginia Tech's national security-focused AI training program concentrates advanced capability development around defense sector needs, potentially deepening the expertise gap between regions with federal/defense connections and those without.
